heading: National asset database
label: 664 National asset database

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary, in consultation with appropriate homeland security officials of the States, determines to be vital and the loss, interruption, incapacity, or destruction of which would have a negative or debilitating effect on the economic security, public health, or safety of the United States, any State, or any local government; or

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary determines is appropriate for inclusion in the database.

children.heading: Prioritized critical infrastructure list
children.label: 2 Prioritized critical infrastructure list
children.text: In accordance with Homeland Security Presidential Directive–7, as in effect on , the Secretary shall establish and maintain a single classified prioritized list of systems and assets included in the database under paragraph (1) that the Secretary determines would, if destroyed or disrupted, cause national or regional catastrophic effects.January 1, 2007

children.heading: Use of database
children.label: b Use of database
children.text: The Secretary shall use the database established under subsection (a)(1) in the development and implementation of Department plans and programs as appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: establishing data collection guidelines and providing such guidelines to the appropriate homeland security official of each State;

children.label: B
children.text: regularly reviewing the guidelines established under subparagraph (A), including by consulting with the appropriate homeland security officials of States, to solicit feedback about the guidelines, as appropriate;

children.label: C
children.text: after providing the homeland security official of a State with the guidelines under subparagraph (A), allowing the official a reasonable amount of time to submit to the Secretary any data submissions recommended by the official for inclusion in the database established under subsection (a)(1);

children.label: D
children.text: examining the contents and identifying any submissions made by such an official that are described incorrectly or that do not meet the guidelines established under subparagraph (A); and

children.label: E
children.text: providing to the appropriate homeland security official of each relevant State a list of submissions identified under subparagraph (D) for review and possible correction before the Secretary finalizes the decision of which submissions will be included in the database established under subsection (a)(1).

children.label: A
children.text: according to the sectors listed in National Infrastructure Protection Plan developed pursuant to Homeland Security Presidential Directive–7; and

children.label: B
children.text: by the State and county of their location.

children.heading: Private sector integration
children.label: 3 Private sector integration
children.text: The Secretary shall identify and evaluate methods, including the Department’s Protected Critical Infrastructure Information Program, to acquire relevant private sector information for the purpose of using that information to generate any database or list, including the database established under subsection (a)(1) and the list established under subsection (a)(2).

children.heading: Retention of classification
children.label: 4 Retention of classification
children.text: The classification of information required to be provided to Congress, the Department, or any other department or agency under this section by a Sector Risk Management Agency, including the assignment of a level of classification of such information, shall be binding on Congress, the Department, and that other Federal agency.

children.heading: Report required
children.label: 1 Report required
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , and annually thereafter, the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs of the Senate and the Committee on Homeland Security of the House of Representatives a report on the database established under subsection (a)(1) and the list established under subsection (a)(2).August 3, 2007

children.label: A
children.text: The name, location, and sector classification of each of the systems and assets on the list established under subsection (a)(2).

children.label: B
children.text: The name, location, and sector classification of each of the systems and assets on such list that are determined by the Secretary to be most at risk to terrorism.

children.label: C
children.text: Any significant challenges in compiling the list of the systems and assets included on such list or in the database established under subsection (a)(1).

children.label: D
children.text: Any significant changes from the preceding report in the systems and assets included on such list or in such database.

children.label: E
children.text: If appropriate, the extent to which such database and such list have been used, individually or jointly, for allocating funds by the Federal Government to prevent, reduce, mitigate, or respond to acts of terrorism.

children.label: F
children.text: The amount of coordination between the Department and the private sector, through any entity of the Department that meets with representatives of private sector industries for purposes of such coordination, for the purpose of ensuring the accuracy of such database and such list.

children.label: G
children.text: Any other information the Secretary deems relevant.

children.heading: Classified information
children.label: 3 Classified information
children.text: The report shall be submitted in unclassified form but may contain a classified annex.

be composed of national laboratories, Federal agencies, State and local homeland security organizations, academic institutions, or national Centers of Excellence that have demonstrated experience working with and identifying critical infrastructure and key resources; and
provide input to the Secretary on any request pertaining to the contents of such database or such list.
